title: Refuge
content:
From my spiritï¿½s gray defeat,
From my pulseï¿½s flagging beat,
From my hopes that turned to sand
Sifting through my close-clenched hand,
From my own faultï¿½s slavery,
If I can sing, I still am free.

For with my singing I can make
A refuge for my spiritï¿½s sake,
A house of shining words, to be
My fragile immortality.
meaning:
Sara Teasdale's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Refuge"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
